stem: A software testing study surveys 15 test cases and observes 4 positive outcomes out of 15 (26.7%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (8 observations, rate = 12.5%) and Group B (7 observations, rate = 42.9%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 32% and 51% of the population. Estimate the true software bug rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.2667. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.3019 (range [0.2748, 0.3321]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.109, 0.5195]. Point estimate: 0.3019.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.42: 0.42×0.127 + 0.58×0.4286 = 0.3019.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=15, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
